sourceMovie.title: Steamboy
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Steamboy (2004) stuck because it commits to its animation premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Katsuhiro Otomo's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The animation and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Origin: Spirits of the Past
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Steamboy clicked for you, Origin: Spirits of the Past (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and fantasy, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Robots
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Steamboy clicked for you, Robots (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and science fiction, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Degeneration
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Steamboy clicked for you, Resident Evil: Degeneration (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Space Pirate Captain Harlock
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Steamboy clicked for you, Space Pirate Captain Harlock (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and science fiction, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Steamboy?
faq.answer: Try Origin: Spirits of the Past, Robots, Resident Evil: Degeneration—each shares DNA with Steamboy while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Steamboy?
faq.answer: Steamboy (2004) is best described as Animation, Fantasy, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Steamboy worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
